title: Housing-application questions — five-roommate lease scoping in Corvallis
html:
TL;DR

  • Mid-March 2022 rapid-fire question list Yahir jotted while shopping for a five-person lease in Corvallis during sophomore year at OSU.
  • Five concrete due-diligence questions for a landlord/property manager: market timing, application-to-signing window, co-signer requirements, what happens if not all roommates have jobs, what happens after the application + deposit are submitted.
  • Title — "How people have to apply, all five apply" — is the operative constraint: the lease wants every roommate underwritten individually, which is the friction point Yahir is trying to solve.
  • Pair with the "five people rooming" lyric note from three weeks earlier, where the same five-roommate decision is sitting next to a Mac Miller lyric about pain — the housing logistics and the late-night spiral were running concurrently.
  • Single-source artifact of the OSU sophomore housing scramble (March 2022) that anyone reading the Corvallis cluster should land on for context.

Key fragments

"Will more homes be on the market in the near future on average"

"How many co-signer are we going to need on a lease"

"People who don't have jobs can they still apply"

Why it matters

Cross-reference with 2022 02 26 2009 mac miller lyrics five people rooming — same five-roommate decision, three weeks apart, completely different headspaces. This note is calm-checklist-Yahir; the Mac Miller note is 4:51am pain-spiral-Yahir. Same problem.

Open questions

  • Did the five-roommate house actually happen, and if so where in Corvallis?
  • Who were the other four — does this overlap with the OSIG crowd or the corvallis crypto club orbit?

Wikilinks
